basic_info.headline: Frontier Red Team, Cyber Lead at Anthropic
basic_info.about:
I currently lead the cyber effort on the Frontier Red Team at Anthropic, where I try to better understand the cyber-relevant risks of AI systems, and build evaluations to measure those risks. Previously at Anthropic, I worked on various aspects of finetuning, evaluations, and red teaming.

Prior to my current work, I completed a PhD in theoretical physics working at the intersection of quantum information and quantum gravity.

experience.title: Graduate Researcher
experience.description:
- Advised by Raphael Bousso; conducting research at the intersection of quantum information,
quantum gravity, quantum error correction, and quantum computing
- Research focuses on translating complex models into simpler, interpretable ones that are more
computationally tractable and easier to interpret and understand, as well as how to translate
results on toy models to the original, more complicated system
- Research uses a variety of interdisciplinary tools across math and physics, including linear algebra,
calculus, random matrix theory, information theory, and probability
- Planned and executed novel research projects, yielding 4 publications in peer-reviewed journals
with 3 forthcoming, both independently and in collaboration
- 12 talks and presentations to academic and non-academic audiences from 2019 to present

experience.title: Head Graduate Student Instructor
experience.description:
- develop original teaching materials, as well as planning and executing lessons to assist in the delivery of beginner to advanced physics material
- manage a team of 8+ Graduate Student Instructors in executing novel virtual teaching materials to courses of over 400 students
- assisted in the transition to remote teaching, and the development of virtual resources
- Course History: Head GSI 7A & 7B (Physics for Scientists and Engineers, Mechanics, EM & Thermodynamics), 5A (Honors Introductory Mechanics and Relativity), 105 (Analytic Mechanics), 137B (Quantum Mechanics II), 221B (Graduate Quantum Mechanics II)

experience.title: Quantitative Research Intern
experience.location: New York, New York, United States

experience.title: Research Assistant
experience.description:
- worked with Shamit Kachru studying string theory
- research into moonshine and related number-theoretic aspects of string theory
- research and honors thesis on applying the attractor mechanism to string-y construction of extremal Kerr black holes

experience.title: Math and Physics Tutor
experience.description:
- provided both private and group tutoring for introductory physics and mathematics courses
- created original materials for exam preparation
- received training in modern, research-driven STEM tutoring/teaching practices
- assisted with practical interviews for new tutor applicants

experience.title: Mentor
experience.description:
- combined resident assistant and tutor for Stanford High School Summer College
- provided academic assistance for high school students taking summer classes at Stanford
- worked with other Mentors to manage and supervise high school students in dormitories
- planned and executed residential (educational and entertainment) activities for residents

experience.title: Oral Communications Tutor
experience.company: The Hume Center for Writing and Speaking at Stanford University
experience.description:
- provided one-on-one tutoring for undergraduate and graduate oral communication activities, assisting with content, technique, and usage of technology/media
- specialized in science communication
- planned and executed speaking workshops for Program in Writing and Rhetoric courses
